subject: Cloud Computing
title: E-Waste and Hardware Lifecycles
description: A fast breakdown of E-Waste and Hardware Lifecycles for builders.

hook.text: Cloud providers replace servers every 3-5 years. Millions of machines. Where do they all go?

buildup.text: Decommissioned servers contain rare metals, plastics, and hazardous materials. Responsible recycling is expensive.

discovery.text: AWS, Google, and Microsoft have all committed to zero-waste goals. They refurbish, donate, and recycle components.

twist.text: Extending server lifetimes from 3 to 5 years reduces e-waste by 40%. But newer hardware is more energy-efficient.

climax.text: The cloud industry is learning to balance performance upgrades with environmental responsibility. Tension remains.

punchline.text: Millions of servers retired yearly. Recycling must keep pace.

quiz.question: What trade-off exists in extending cloud server lifetimes?
options: Older servers generate more e-waste when replaced but newer servers are more energy-efficient
Older servers are always better in every way than newer ones
Extending server lifetimes has no downsides whatsoever
